headline: How Investors May Respond To Southwest Airlines (LUV) Securing US$500 Million Aircraft‑Backed Term Loan Financing
summary: In March 2026, Southwest Airlines Co. entered into a new US$500 million senior secured term loan facility maturing in 2029, backed by certain aircraft and related assets and carrying interest tied to Term SOFR or an alternate base rate plus a modest margin. By locking in this aircraft‑secured funding while reporting better‑than‑expected Q4 2025 results and stronger 2026 guidance, Southwest is reinforcing both its liquidity position and its capacity plans during a period of meaningful...

headline: Southwest Airlines Fee Shift Tests Brand Loyalty And Investor Demands
summary: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) has dropped its long standing open seating model in favor of new paid seating options. The airline is also introducing checked bag fees, moving away from its two bags fly free policy. These changes follow pressure from activist investor Elliott Management and have triggered backlash from some longtime customers. Investors and customers are watching how the updated model affects brand perception and loyalty. Southwest has built its identity as a low cost,...
